questions: hawala ar bank transfer er difference ki?
answers: Hawala is an informal money transfer system where money is transferred through a network of brokers (hawaladars) without actually moving physical currency across borders.
Hawala holo ekta informal taka pathano system ja brokers er network diye kaje kore. Taka physically move kore na, but duipashe brokers settle kore.
In hawala, you give money to a broker in one country who contacts a broker in the destination country to pay the recipient. Settlement between brokers happens later.
Hawala is illegal or heavily regulated in most countries because it operates outside the banking system and can be used for money laundering, though historically it served legitimate purposes.
